mnemonics: GASPS CASH: Gradual decrease in spatial dimensions (from 32x32 to a vector) and a corresponding increase in the depth (number of channels) from 1 to 10.
keyPoints: Pioneering CNN architecture by Yann LeCun for handwritten digit recognition.
Uses a sequence of Convolution and Subsampling (Pooling) layers.
Employs tanh and sigmoid activations instead of modern ReLU.
Ends with fully connected layers for classification.
The '5' stands for 5 trainable layers (2 convolutional, 3 fully connected).
commonMistakes: Assuming it uses ReLU activation (it used tanh/sigmoid).
Forgetting that average pooling was used, not max pooling.
Misidentifying the number of layers (5 trainable, 7 total with pooling).
